name: Royal Conservatory Orchestra with Conductor Earl Lee

location.name: Koerner Hall, Royal Conservatory of Music

description:
A renowned Korean-Canadian cellist and conductor, currently Music Director of the Ann Arbor Symphony. He also served as assistant conductor of the Boston Symphony, resident conductor of the Toronto Symphony Orchestra, and associate conductor of the Pittsburgh Symphony. He is the 2022 recipient of The Sir Georg Solti Conducting Award.

The Temerty Orchestral Program at The Glenn Gould School, the Royal Conservatory Orchestra is widely regarded as an outstanding ensemble and one of the best training orchestras in North America, with graduates having joined the ranks of the greatest orchestras in the world.

Please join us! This concert is preceded by a short performance by talented students of The Royal Conservatory.

name: The Toronto Tattoo Show/NIX 2026
description:
Celebrate 26 years of ink at Canada's longest-running and most iconic tattoo convention!

Whether you're looking to get tattooed by a top artist, discover the latest in tattoo culture, or just immerse yourself in the inked lifestyle, this is the place to be.

Enjoy live tattooing, vendors, art, entertainment, contests and much more all weekend long. Bookings and walk-up appointments available.

Don't miss this legendary 3-day celebration of art, expression, and community.

name: Footsteps Around the Fort - Fort York Neighbourhood Tours

location.name: Tours run at 11 a.m. and 2 p.m. from Canoe Landing Park, Bobber Plaza

description:
Discover the hidden gems and iconic landmarks of the CityPlace Fort York neighbourhood. This guided tour gives you a new perspective on the evolving landscape of this community. Discover how historic landscapes continue to shape Toronto today as stories come to life through storytelling and conversation along the route. Come dressed for the weather. Perfect for families and dog-friendly, all ages are welcome to learn, explore and experience the neighbourhood together. 

The tour route covers about 2 km over paved pathways and includes major intersections.
